rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Write output dependency guarantees in component type annexes using only visible inputs and outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Use component type annexes for type-level contracts and implementation annexes for implementation-specific bindings
Ensure all referenced identifiers in AGREE annexes are visible in the current scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not delete generated clauses without validating the requirement and AADL data representation
